channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden eulogizes Ethel Kennedy as 'hero' who put her own stamp on country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden eulogized the late Ethel Kennedy in deeply personal terms at a memorial service Wednesday, hailing the wife of the late Sen. Robert F.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman alleges Diddy violently 'gang raped' her in Orinda as form of 'payback'
item.description: A California woman has filed a new lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs, alleging that he and his entourage sexually assaulted her in 2018 as an act of retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: A Texas board rejects clemency plea from a man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas state board on Wednesday declined to stop what could be the first execution in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC overrules own sanctuary policy, hands migrant teen convicted in attack on cops to ICE
item.description: An illegal immigrant was arrested by immigration authorities upon his release from a New York City jail after a conviction for attacking a pair of police officers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia residents complain of squatters in home owned by realty agency: 'It's a problem'
item.description: Angry residents at a housing complex in an Atlanta suburb said a handful of squatters have taken over a home in the area, leading to HOA rule violations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Brightest supermoon rising this week, and it won't be alone
item.description: The third of four supermoons this year, October’s full moon, known as the Hunter’s Moon, will illuminate the sky on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: “Lyft believes transportation access should never be a barrier for any citizen seeking to vote," Lyft Chief Policy Officer Jerry Golden said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Killer mom Susan Smith disciplined behind bars weeks before parole hearing
item.description: Convicted child killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a new disciplinary charge after speaking with a documentary filmmaker, weeks before her first parole hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Witnesses saw an armed group harassing Helene aid workers in a small Tennessee town, sheriff says
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Witnesses reported seeing a group of armed people harassing hurricane relief workers in a remote Tennessee community last weekend, a sheriff said Wednesday as a man in North Carolina appeared in court for allegedly threatening aid workers in that state.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Menendez brothers inch closer to freedom from 'life without parole' for parents' Beverly Hills murders
item.description: Supporters of Lyle and Erik Menendez rally outside Los Angeles' Hall of Justice to call for a new sentence and release from prison after the 1989 murders of their parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge in Trump election case orders prosecutors to look for, produce info from Pence documents probe
item.description: The federal judge overseeing the election interference case against Donald Trump has directed prosecutors to search for and provide to the former president’s lawyers any Justice Department information related to a separate investigation into Mike Pence’s handling of classified documents.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Killer's escape from Pennsylvania jail costs him his right to appeal life sentence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A convicted killer’s escape from a Pennsylvania jail last year has cost him his right to appeal his original life sentence, a state court ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mormon voters set to play critical role in swing states
item.description: Mormon voters are increasingly seen as a crucial swing vote in the presidential election, with both Vice President Harris and former President Trump stepping up their outreach to the demographic in multiple battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois teen deemed ineligible for cross-country race after charity 5K
item.description: “I don’t really think there’s any reason that running a charity 5K should get me disqualified for running cross-country meets,” freshman Cole Deering said.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Bernthal, NewsNation

item.title: Arctic fox found stranded in Portland now receiving medical care
item.description: “While we don’t have information yet on where the Arctic fox came from, or how she got to Portland,” Bird Alliance of Oregon spokesperson Ali Berman said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Pieces of Tropicana to be restored, preserved at Neon Museum
item.description: "Vegas is a city that constantly builds up and blows up," Aaron Berger, Executive Director of the Neon Museum Las Vegas said.
item.dc:creator: Madison Kimbro, NewsNation

item.title: Former Las Vegas-area official sentenced in murder of journalist
item.description: The former public administrator was accused of killing Jeff German after the reporter wrote articles critical of his conduct in office.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honey prices increase as Rio Grande Valley bees struggle
item.description: "It's very challenging with all the new problems we’ve had from imported pests and diseases," says Brownsville beekeeper Glenn Simpson of Bay Bees RGV.
item.dc:creator: Blake Reagan Brown, NewsNation

item.title: California health care workers get a pay bump under a new minimum wage law
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Some of the lowest-paid health care workers in California will get a pay bump Wednesday under a state law gradually increasing their wages to at least $25 an hour.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, a tech mogul who is the world's richest person, plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures re
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Defense contractor RTX agrees to pay more than $950 million to resolve bribery, fraud claims
item.description: RTX Corporation, the defense contractor formerly known as Raytheon, has agreed to pay more than $950 million to resolve allegations that it defrauded the government and paid bribes to secure business with Qatar.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump: Black and Hispanic voters who vote for Harris 'gotta have your head examined'
item.description: The former president suggested any Black or Hispanic voters who do not support him should have their head examined, echoing a similar comment he has made about Jewish voters backing Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Arizona man who admitted to murdering his wife receives his sentence
item.description: Daniel Paduchowski was sentenced to 16 years in prison after admitting to the murder of his wife, Kelly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California man accused of assaulting, tying up homeowner during burglary
item.description: A California man was arrested on accusations that he assaulted a man and tied him up in his own home during a home invasion in Riverside County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California judge who allegedly killed his wife continues to receive nearly $250K salary
item.description: A California judge who is custody for allegedly killing his wife is still receiving his nearly $250,000 salary, in accordance with the state Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Injured Alex Cobb dropped from Guardians' ALCS roster, replaced by Ben Lively
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pitcher Alex Cobb was dropped from Cleveland's American League Championship Series roster on Tuesday after he got hurt yet again in the opener, leaving the Guardians in search of a Game 5 starter against the New York Yankees. “It’s been a challenging year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico's former public security chief set to be sentenced in US drug case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mexico's former public security chief is set to be sentenced in a U.S. court on Wednesday after being convicted of taking bribes to aid drug traffickers.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Super Bowl will return to Atlanta in 2028: Where will it be played before that?
item.description: Atlanta has secured the 2028 Super Bowl - here are the other cities that will host the NFL's championship game before then.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Some Social Security recipients will receive an extra check next month
item.description: It may be hard to believe, but November is fast approaching. With it will come Election Day, Thanksgiving, and, for some seven million Supplemental Security Income beneficiaries, an extra check. It's all thanks to a quirk in the 2024 calendar.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Mexican court workers block traffic at US port of entry
item.description: Dozens of demonstrators carrying banners and signs on Tuesday morning blocked passenger vehicle traffic on the Juarez, Mexico, side of the Bridge of the Americas.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: Trump and his Republican allies contend these ballots could be part of an elaborate scheme to steal the election from him, a claim for which there is no evidence. Their challenge comes as the voters who receive the ballots are increasingly from groups that are presumed to be Democratic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Doubs catches 2 TD passes in his return from a suspension to help Packers rout Cardinals 34-13
item.description: Romeo Doubs wanted to make amends after serving a one-game suspension for what the Green Bay Packers described as conduct detrimental to the team.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5
